key_insight: Fine-tuning a foundation ML interatomic potential can recover the barocaloric phase transformation of ammonium sulfate with only 5–10 DFT structures, whereas training from scratch needs much more data.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical route to cheaper, faster screening of solid-state refrigerants by reducing the DFT cost of training atomistic models for phase-behavior simulations.
method: Compared three MACE-based MLIP strategies: training from scratch, naive replay fine-tuning, and multihead replay fine-tuning of MACE-MPA-0.
Evaluated models in molecular dynamics simulations across different training-set sizes for their ability to reproduce the temperature-driven structural transformation.
Tested whether hybrid-DFT training and dispersion correction improve phase-behavior fidelity.
evidence_signal: The fine-tuned models reproduced the ammonium sulfate phase transformation with as few as 5 to 10 60-atom DFT configurations, while the foundation model alone and small-data scratch models failed.
watch_item: Performance appears system-specific, and correct phase behavior required including dispersion correction; extrapolation outside the fine-tuning domain may be less reliable for naive replay than multihead replay.
paper_title: Barocaloric phase transformation from data efficient fine-tuning of machine learned interatomic potentials
